title: Forkly Empty Cart Bug
candidatePrompt: Sam, your engineering manager, says: "Some users say their cart is empty when they come back. Can you look into it?" You have 5 questions before recommending what to investigate or fix.

persona.name: Sam
persona.answerStyle: answers exactly what is asked; does not connect all dots; kind but not handholding
expertise: coordinating debugging across app and backend owners
hedgedKnowledge: whether the recent release is causal
communicationRules: does not diagnose root cause unless asked toward it
hedges technical details outside direct ownership
does not recommend rollback without evidence

ambientFacts.fact: Two engineers touched checkout recently, but nobody is assigned to this yet.
Cart state has both local app state and server-side persistence for logged-in users.
The first reports came from customer support tickets.

hiddenFacts.title: iOS only
hiddenFacts.fact: The issue only appears in iOS reports; Android and web do not show the same pattern.
hiddenFacts.sampleResponse: The reports we have are iOS. I have not seen the same pattern on Android or web.
hiddenFacts.whyItMatters: Narrows the investigation away from backend-only explanations.

hiddenFacts.title: Guest users affected
hiddenFacts.fact: The issue affects guest users, not logged-in users.
hiddenFacts.sampleResponse: It looks concentrated among guest users. Logged-in users seem mostly fine.
hiddenFacts.whyItMatters: Points to local/session behavior rather than persisted cart records.

hiddenFacts.title: After backgrounding
hiddenFacts.fact: Carts vanish after the app is backgrounded for more than 30 minutes.
hiddenFacts.sampleResponse: The rough repro is guest user adds items, backgrounds the app for 30-plus minutes, then returns.
hiddenFacts.whyItMatters: Reveals the actual condition needed to reproduce the bug.

hiddenFacts.title: Recent UI release is a trap
hiddenFacts.fact: A UI-only release shipped three days ago, but it is likely coincidental and not the root cause.
hiddenFacts.sampleResponse: There was a UI-only release three days ago. It is tempting to blame that, but I am not sure it touches cart persistence.
hiddenFacts.whyItMatters: Tests anchoring resistance; rollback is tempting but likely wrong.

hiddenFacts.title: Session/cache expiry root cause
hiddenFacts.fact: The likely root cause is guest cart session/cache expiry after inactivity.
hiddenFacts.sampleResponse: My hunch is guest cart persistence or session expiry after inactivity. Logged-in carts are server-backed.

trapAssumptions.assumption: The candidate assumes the recent UI release caused the cart loss and recommends rollback.
trapAssumptions.whyTempting: The timeline overlaps with the first reports.
trapAssumptions.howToDisprove: Ask about affected platform, user segment, repro steps, and whether the changed code touches cart persistence.

idealRecommendation: Investigate iOS guest cart persistence after backgrounding for 30+ minutes. Do not immediately roll back the UI release unless evidence ties it to cart persistence. Reproduce with guest users, inspect local/session expiry, and patch persistence or restore cart state on return.
